summary: Policy-backed control for whether the Hibernate option is shown in the power menu. Useful for managed or simplified menus, but it removes a visible path to hibernation.

setting.name: Show Hibernate Option
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can show or hide Hibernate in the power menu. This policy decides whether that shortcut is visible.
setting.why_people_change_it: People hide it when they want a simpler power menu or when hibernation should not be offered as a visible user choice.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Hiding Hibernate simplifies the menu, but it also removes one direct way to enter hibernation from the power menu.
setting.professional_notes: WindowsExplorer.admx maps ShowHibernateOption under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. WindowsExplorer.adml says enabled shows Hibernate when supported by the hardware, disabled hides it, and not configured lets users choose through Power Options Control Panel. The local Power.txt trace also lists AllowHibernate, HiberFileSizePercent, and HiberFileType in the power block, which are about hibernation support itself rather than the Explorer menu visibility switch.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force Hibernate visibility and users can choose through the normal controls.

allowed_values.label: Hibernate option hidden
allowed_values.meaning: The Hibernate option is never shown in the power menu.

allowed_values.label: Hibernate option shown
allowed_values.meaning: The Hibernate option is shown in the power menu when the hardware supports it.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes ShowHibernateOption = 0, which matches the documented hide behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 8 and later systems exposing the Explorer policy
windows_defaults.notes: Not configured leaves Hibernate visibility under the normal Windows controls.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: Managed systems that want Hibernate hidden
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep the normal Hibernate visibility behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Hibernate removed from the power menu.
tradeoffs: Hibernate can remain visible where supported.
No forced menu change is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Hide hibernate menu entry
intended_for: Systems that should not advertise Hibernate in the power menu
avoid_for: Users who want quick access to Hibernate

states.rationale: Hide the Hibernate option from the power menu.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a simpler power menu or do not want users choosing Hibernate from that menu.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Hibernate to stay easy to access from the power menu.
tradeoffs: The Hibernate shortcut disappears from the menu.
Users need another path if they still want Hibernate.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsExplorer.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps ShowHibernateOption under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsExplorer.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy shows Hibernate when supported, disabling it hides Hibernate, and not configuring it leaves the choice to the user through the normal controls.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes ShowHibernateOption = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: nohuto power trace for hibernation support
evidence.summary: Power.txt lists AllowHibernate, HiberFileSizePercent, and HiberFileType in the power values block, which are adjacent to the Explorer ShowHibernateOption policy but govern hibernation support itself.

decision.why: Local Microsoft policy files document the exact policy surface, value semantics, and user-facing behavior. The app writes the documented hide state.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="ShowHibernateOption" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer" valueName="ShowHibernateOption">; WindowsExplorer.adml: If you disable this policy setting, the hibernate option will never be shown in the Power Options menu.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact key and value name; local ADML confirms that disabling the policy hides Hibernate from the Power Options menu.
